case_name: David Angel Sifuentes, III v. Christian Brothers Automotive
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff relied on a nonexistent case citation; court noted 'Bolin v. Story, 225 F.3d 1234, 1239 (6th Cir. 2000)' does not exist and had been previously warned.
summary: The court identified that Plaintiff relied on a nonexistent case citation and noted the citation had been previously flagged in the record.

case_name: Ross Logan v. LVNV Funding et al.
Coleman & Lewis v. PNC Bank, N.A.
hallucination_items: False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiffs cited non-existent quotes attributed to Medrano v. Flagstar Bank; the Court flagged these as inaccurate/AI-hallucinated. || False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiffs cited non-existent quotes attributed to Guerra v. Just Mortgage, Inc.; the Court identified these as inaccurate/AI-hallucinated. || False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiffs attributed non-existent quotations to Collins v. Union Fed. Sav. & Loan Ass'n; the Court noted the citation contained incorrect or fabricated quoted material. || False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiffs attributed non-existent quotations to Chapman v. Deutsche Bank Nat'l Trust Co.; the Court marked these as inaccurate/AI-hallucinated citations. || Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiffs fabricated an entire case, citing 'Estate of Bowles v. Comm'r, 608 F. Appx. 693 (9th Cir. 2015)'; the Court found this citation was fabricated/misattributed (not a 9th Cir. opinion) and noted the nearby real citation was United States v. Martinez, 608 Fed. Appx. 692 (10th Cir. 2015).

case_name: Lowrey v. City of Rio Rancho
hallucination_items: Misrepresented: Case Law | Plaintiff cited Creekmore v. Food Lion, Inc., asserting it supports a rule about incomplete state-court records invalidating removal; the Court found that Creekmore does not support that proposition and Plaintiff misrepresented its holding. || Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited 'Cornell v. Precision Valve & Automation, Inc., 2011 WL 1420182 (D.N.M. 2011)' repeatedly; the Court was unable to find the case and concluded it is fictitious. || Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff relied on 'Baldwin v. Perdue Farms, Inc., 569 F. Supp. 2d 754 (E.D. Va. 2008)'; the Court could not locate this authority and concluded it is nonexistent. || Misrepresented: Case Law | Plaintiff quoted and over-extended a portion of Getty Oil's footnote and represented it as binding Tenth Circuit law; the Court found this characterization misleading. || Misrepresented: Case Law | Plaintiff attributed a non-existent quoted holding to Cornwall v. Robinson and represented it as Tenth Circuit precedent; the Court noted the quoted language does not appear in Cornwall and the issue was not decided there. || Misrepresented: Case Law | Plaintiff cited several Supreme Court and circuit opinions (e.g., Martin v. Franklin Capital, Carnegie-Mellon, Gibbs, Merrell Dow, Steel Co.) and misapplied or overstated their holdings for the proposition that removal should be invalidated here; the Court found those characterizations misleading.

case_name: Borsody v. Frontier Heritage Communities
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff's response cited 'Alford v. City of Canon City, 2019 WL 13202842, at *3 (D. Colo. Mar. 29, 2019),' which neither the Court nor defense counsel could locate; Court treated it as a non-existent citation and admonished plaintiff to verify AI-generated citations.

case_name: Kheir v. Titan Team, The Money Source Inc., and Auction.com
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited Brasher v. Stewart; Court found no such case and the citation points to an unrelated Sabine Pilot Serv. decision; quoted text not present. || False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiff quoted language attributed to Reinagel; Court found the quoted text does not appear in Reinagel and the actual holding differs. || False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiff attributed a definitive statement about 'void assignments' to Morlock; Court found the quoted language not present and that Morlock addressed non-borrower standing. || False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiff attributed liability language to Miller regarding trustee liability under Tex. Prop. Code §51.007(f); Court found Miller did not contain that language or interpret §51.007(f). || False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiff cited Slaughter v. Qualls for a requirement tied to the Texas Property Code; Court noted the Texas Property Code was not enacted until 1984 and the quoted text is not in Slaughter. || Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited DTND Sierra Invs., LLC v. BNY Mellon; Court found no such decision at the cited reporter/location and the citation corresponded to unrelated authority; quoted language not found. || Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited Gossett v. Fed. Home Loan Mortg. Corp. with a quoted passage; Court found the quoted text not present in the actual Gossett decision and the citation did not support the quoted language.
outcome: Costs Order; 6 hours CLE on generative AI; provide order to client; Bar referral.

case_name: Lnu, et al. v. Bondi
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law || Fabricated: Case Law || False Quotes: Case Law || False Quotes: Case Law

case_name: Alaya Coleman v. RPF-Somers Investors, LLC, et al.
hallucination_items: False Quotes: Case Law | The Removing Defendants contended that Plaintiff 'hallucinates nonexistent quotes from cases' in support of her remand argument; the Court agreed the cited authority was inapplicable but did not identify or quote a specific fabricated citation.

case_name: Todd E. Glass v. Foley & Lardner LLP
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Court could not verify the citation; the case 'S.G. v. SafeSport, 74 F.4th 864, 870 (7th Cir. 2023)' does not appear to exist. || Fabricated: Case Law | Court could not locate this district decision; 'Cafasso v. Central Refrigerated Servs., Inc., 486 F. Supp. 2d 1077, 1084 (W.D. Wis. 2007)' does not appear to exist in this district or under that reporter information. || Misrepresented: Case Law | Citation appears misrepresented: the cited case number 'Cressey v. Hamilton, No. 21-CV-2031 (D. Kan. 2021)' corresponds to a different matter in the District of Kansas.

case_name: Xavier Jamal Smith v. Santander Consumer USA Inc.
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited 'Citizens Nat’l Bank of Paris v. Midwest Agri-Dev. Corp., 887 F.2d 1128, 1134 (7th Cir. 1989)'; the Court found the reporter citation corresponds to an unrelated First Circuit opinion, no Seventh Circuit case by that name from 1989 appears to exist, and it could not locate any case containing the quoted language.
summary: Rather amazingly, plaintiff asked and was granted leave to file a "Case Law Verification Index" that contained further hallucinations.

case_name: I.H. v. O.K.
hallucination_items: Misrepresented: Case Law | Appellant's brief contained multiple authorities that did not support the propositions for which they were cited; the court attributed this to AI use and warned of risks.

case_name: Wheat v. Vichie
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff's papers cite 'Sosnovska v. Belle World Beauty, Inc.' as a First Department decision; the court found the case to be non-existent and warned that further false representations could result in sanctions. || Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff's papers cite 'Sosnovska v. Belle World Beauty, Inc.' as a Second Department decision; the court found the citation likewise fictional and cautioned the pro se litigant.

case_name: Isaacs v. Novartis Pharmaceuticals Corporation
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Counsel cited a non-existent case in a filing; the Court could not locate it and counsel admitted it was provided by the client via ChatGPT. || Fabricated: Case Law | Counsel cited a non-existent case in a filing; the Court could not locate it and counsel admitted it was provided by the client via ChatGPT.
outcome: Court required counsel to certify completion of an AI seminar

case_name: Medical Buyers Group LLC d/b/a Integrity v. Candice Pence, et al.
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Integrity cited Truist Bank v. Buerge, 2022 WL 17541757, at *6 (M.D. Ga. Dec. 8, 2022); court conducted independent search and could not locate the opinion; citation remained in Integrity's final amended response. || Fabricated: Case Law | Alleged citation Mission Critical Sols., LLC v. Connolly, 2021 WL 5985016 (M.D. Ga. Dec. 16, 2021) appeared in earlier filing but was not in the final amended response; court could not locate the opinion in its searches. || Fabricated: Case Law | Alleged citation 'Bliss, 279 Ga. App. At 807' appeared in earlier briefing and could not be located by the court's searches. || Misrepresented: Case Law | Integrity is alleged to have misrepresented the holding of Encompass Off. Sols., Inc. v. Ingenix, Inc.; the court read Encompass and found Defendants' allegation that the holding was misstated to be credible.
summary: Order to show cause is here. Counsel was eventually ordered to pay 10,000 USD to opposing counsel (see here).

case_name: Kaleb Alexander Hoosier v. Executive Centre Association, et al.
hallucination_items: Misrepresented: Legal Norm | Complaint cites "ROH § 14-1" in support of a "public easement sidewalk" proposition; court noted ROH § 14-1 actually concerns use of Polynesian plants and does not support the cited proposition. || Fabricated: Legal Norm | Complaint cites "ROH § 29-7.2" for the proposition that intentional confinement via trespass is unlawful; the court found no such section and observed ROH ch.29 pertains to affordable housing. || Fabricated: Case Law | "Blair v. City of Honolulu" cited in the complaint but does not appear to exist; court flagged it as likely fictitious.

case_name: Attorney Disciplinary Board v. Richard Louis Pazdernik, Jr.
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law || Misrepresented: Case Law

case_name: State ex rel. Soretha Marie Eldridge v. Judge Ashley Kilbane
summary: Relator admitted her petition contained citation errors and hallucinated cases after relying on inadequate assistance; court denied leave to amend and dismissed the writs.

case_name: County of Los Angeles v. Neill Francis Niblett
hallucination_items: Misrepresented: Case Law | Opening brief relied on an excerpt of Scripps Health (pp. 335–336) to assert threats must be immediate; the court explained the cited excerpt does not address that issue. || Fabricated: Case Law | Opening brief cited a non-existent case 'Montebello Unified School District v. State Board of Education (1991) 226 Cal.App.3d 1685'; counsel later conceded in a notice of errata that the citation does not appear to exist and attributed its inclusion to AI editing. || Fabricated: Case Law | Opening brief cited 'R.D. v. P.M. (2021) 68 Cal.App.5th 1012', a citation the County could not locate and the court concluded does not exist as presented. || Misrepresented: Case Law | Counsel misrepresented the holding of United States v. Rahimi, stating it invalidated a federal statute when Rahimi actually upheld the statute as applied in that case. || Misrepresented: Case Law | Counsel cited People v. Zermeno as supporting labor-speech protections and the proposition that strongly worded workplace criticism is protected, but Zermeno concerned gang-enhancement issues and does not support those propositions. || Misrepresented: Case Law | Brief cited Bookout v. Nielsen at page 1143 to claim reversal for lack of immediacy, but Bookout ends at page 1142 and the Court of Appeal affirmed the restraining order; counsel miscited and mischaracterized the case.

case_name: Warner v. Gilbarco, Inc.
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff twice cited 'Smith v. Dow Chem. Co., 166 F.R.D. 1, 2 (E.D. Mich. 1996)' to argue depositions should not proceed; the court found this a 'phantom' citation and noted the actual existing opinion is Hamm v. Nasatka Barriers Inc., 166 F.R.D. 1 (D.D.C. 1996), which does not mention depositions.

case_name: In re: Pamela Williams
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Debtor cited 'White v. Bank of America, 291 Ga. 367 (2012)'; the Court was unable to locate this case and described the citation as incorrect and possibly the product of an AI 'hallucination.'
summary: Debtor cited a case the court could not locate; the court noted the citation appears incorrect and may be an AI "hallucination," and referenced other decisions sanctioning reliance on fabricated AI-generated cases.
